mission.title: kubevirt: Need a way to inject static ip on multus controlled interface
mission.description: Need a way to inject static ip on multus controlled interface. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current kubevirt deployment
steps.description:
Verify your virtctl version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
helm list -n kubevirt 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubevirt installation.

steps.title: Review kubevirt configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubevirt configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl get configmap -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevirt
```
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

**What happened**:
I'm using a bridge interface via multus and I'd like to inject static ip on it.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Need a way to inject static ip on multus controlled…
steps.description:
When virt-controller is generating the Multus pod annotation, several properties can be set:
- `name`
- `namespace`
- `interface`
- `mac` _(optional)_

This proposal enables the customization of the Multus annotation.
```yaml
template:
    spec:
      networks:
      - name: default
        pod: {}
      - name: test
        multus:
          networkName: a-bridge-network
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl get events -n kubevirt --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Need a way to inject static ip on multus controlled…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
When virt-controller is generating the Multus pod annotation, several properties can be set:
- `name`
- `namespace`
- `interface`
- `mac` _(optional)_

This proposal enables the customization of the Multus annotation.
codeSnippets:
template:
    spec:
      networks:
      - name: default
        pod: {}
      - name: test
        multus:
          networkName: a-bridge-network
And then use cloud-init to set your ip in your VM yaml:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevirt installed or the issue environment reproducible.
